supports: Python package claude-agent-sdk and TypeScript package @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk are the supported Agent SDK runtimes
Agent SDK authentication requires an API key or a documented third-party provider
the Agent SDK passes OTel configuration to a Claude Code child process
trace, metric, and log-event exporter labels
content capture is disabled by default and enabled only by opt-in labels
an active application span is documented to propagate TRACEPARENT and TRACESTATE to the child process
Agent SDK Hook callbacks are configured programmatically
Hook lifecycle and input are producer-defined

blocker.evidence: Node.js, Python, and .NET runtimes are available, but repository and installed-package checks found neither @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk nor claude-agent-sdk and found no .NET Agent SDK reference. The installed @anthropic-ai/claude-code 2.1.207 package exposes only the claude CLI and has no Agent SDK dependency. No Agent SDK API-key or documented provider credential was present. No SDK query was started.
blocker.not_substituted: the installed Claude Code CLI, claude -p, an Anthropic client SDK, Managed Agents, or emulated telemetry

named_follow_up.name: #63-live-agent-sdk-inventory
required_preconditions: an already-provided Python claude-agent-sdk or TypeScript @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk package with its exact version recordable
a supported runtime for that package
an Agent SDK API key or documented provider credential authorized for one bounded synthetic query
an already-provided OpenTelemetry SDK capable of creating an active parent span in the selected runtime
a disposable local OTLP capture sink and programmatic Hook callbacks with no persisted raw content
required_execution: start an active application parent span and run one bounded synthetic Agent SDK query with content disabled
run a separate bounded synthetic-marker query with content enabled only if explicitly authorized
record whether the Agent SDK trace is a child of the active parent by byte-equivalent trace context
record only structural OTel and Hook inventories, settings labels, counts, types, and fingerprints
remove only artifacts created by the follow-up
